content.problemStatement:
**This is the easy version of the problem. The only difference between this and hard version is that you don't need to minimize the sum of matrix in this version**

Construct a matrix AAA of size N∗MN*MN∗M such that the GCD of every row and column is 111 and element must be 2<=Ai,j<=1092<=A_{i, j}<=10^{9}2<=Ai,j​<=109.

If there are multiple answers possible, print any.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of single line of input.

The first line of each test case contains two space-separated integers NNN and MMM — the number of rows and columns in the matrix, respectively.




Output Format
For each test case, print a matrix of size N∗MN*MN∗M.
constraints: The sum of N⋅MN\cdot MN⋅M over all test cases won't exceed 3⋅1053 \cdot 10^53⋅105.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
In the first case, there are 2 rows and 3 columns

GCD of 1st row = GCD(9,7,8) = 1
GCD of 2nd row = GCD(4,10,13) = 1
GCD of 1st column = GCD(9,4) = 1
GCD of 2nd column = GCD(7,10) = 1
GCD of 3rd column = GCD(8,13) = 1
